strengths: Signature change is correctly additive: tools=[], toolBudget=3, onToolEvent? defaults preserve all legacy callers (llm.ts:249-251); R1 test confirms no `tools` param leaks into the single-shot path.
Clean separation into executeCardSingleShot vs executeCardWithTools keeps the legacy path byte-for-byte unchanged and isolates the loop complexity (llm.ts:290-326 vs 330-477).

Final-turn-only output is correct: interim turn text routes through onToolEvent({phase:'reasoning'}) (llm.ts:377-379) and only the terminal non-tool_use turn's buffered text becomes output (llm.ts:469-473). R4 test asserts 'let me search' is excluded.

Budget counts model-issued requests not retries: remainingBudget decrements once per executed tool_use block (llm.ts:399); retries are internal to tool.execute. R2 test confirms exactly 1 execution under budget=1.
Over-budget requests are refused with a synthesized 'search limit reached' tool_result while still feeding the loop to a final answer (llm.ts:454-461).
Tool failure feeds a structured error back via tool_result with is_error and fires onToolEvent failed carrying errorCode; loop continues without throwing (llm.ts:440-453). R3 test confirms.
Manual loop using client.messages.stream + finalMessage with hand-built assistant/user tool_result messages; the beta toolRunner is correctly NOT used.

Iteration cap deviation (2*toolBudget+1 instead of the spec's literal toolBudget+1) is a justified correctness fix: the literal value would truncate the loop before the budget-refusal-then-final-answer path the spec's own R2 scenario requires. All 14 tests pass.

issues.description: Defensive UNKNOWN_TOOL handling path (resolving an unrecognized tool name from toolsByName) is beyond the literal DELIVERABLE scenarios. It is harmless and reasonable, but slightly exceeds the minimal spec; worth a glance to confirm it is intended.

The iteration-cap comment ('toolBudget executions + refused retries + one final text turn') under-describes the actual 2*toolBudget+1 bound; the cap is purely defensive and does not strictly bound refused requests to toolBudget. Cosmetic comment precision only.
